question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query object with all three of lower, inner, and upper filter arrays populated simultaneously, causing getQuery() to generate three separate subqueries each with a full LEFT JOIN against the entity table, all UNION ALL'd together with redundant ORDER BY and LIMIT clauses at both subquery and outer level, resulting in at least 3x the normal database processing cost per single API request and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a large spenderIdInFilters array to getSubQuery(), causing the IN clause to expand to dozens of values that are replicated identically across all three subqueries generated by getQuery(), multiplying the per-row filter evaluation cost by 3x and driving sustained CPU consumption on the database processing node above the 30% threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set the limit parameter to its maximum allowed value so that each of the three subqueries (lower, inner, upper) independently returns up to limit rows before the outer UNION ALL sort and re-limit, causing the database to sort and process up to 3*limit rows per request, increasing memory and CPU consumption on the processing node by more than 30% compared to a default-limit request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user pass both a large spenderIdFilters array and a large spenderIdInFilters array simultaneously to getSubQuery(), causing filters.push(...spenderIdFilters) to append many range conditions and the IN clause to expand to many values, creating a WHERE clause so complex that the query planner abandons index usage and performs a sequential scan of the nft table, spiking CPU and I/O on the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path in getQuery() (lines 122-123) by providing non-empty lower, inner, and upper filter arrays, where each subquery independently executes ORDER BY token_id, serial_number and LIMIT, causing the database to perform three separate sort operations plus one outer sort on the merged result set, consuming significantly more CPU than a single-subquery request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user invoke getNfts() with a query that triggers the maximum-complexity path in getQuery() (all three subqueries, large spenderIdInFilters, maximum limit), causing getRows() via pool.queryQuietly() to hold a database connection for an extended duration, exhausting the connection pool and degrading processing capacity for all concurrent requests by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spenderIdInFilters with many duplicate spender ID values to getSubQuery(), causing the IN clause to contain redundant entries that are replicated across all three subqueries, forcing the database to evaluate a bloated IN list per row in each subquery and increasing per-request CPU consumption above the 30% threshold without issuing multiple requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where lower, inner, and upper each contain the maximum number of allowed filter entries, causing the params array shared across all three getSubQuery() calls to grow to a very large size, increasing query plan parsing overhead and memory allocation on the database processing node beyond the 30% consumption threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNft()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getNft() with valid but distinct tokenId and serialNumber combinations that are known to exist, causing nftByIdQuery (select * from nft where token_id = $1 and serial_number = $2) to execute many times in rapid succession, where the select * projection including the metadata column causes large row data transfers that cumulatively increase network and processing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft filter values for token_id and serial_number ranges in the lower, inner, and upper arrays that intentionally span the entire valid range of the nft table, causing each of the three subqueries to perform a full sequential scan of the nft table joined with the entity table, tripling the I/O and CPU cost compared to a targeted query and exceeding the 30% resource consumption increase threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the same spenderIdInFilters and spenderIdFilters arrays are passed to all three getSubQuery() calls (lines 96-104) without copying, so that a large spender filter set is evaluated three times in three separate SQL subqueries within a single request, tripling the filter evaluation cost and increasing node CPU consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spenderIdFilters with many overlapping range conditions (e.g., spender >= 1, spender >= 2, spender >= 3, ...) that are all pushed into the filters array via filters.push(...spenderIdFilters) and appended as separate AND conditions, creating a WHERE clause with many redundant range predicates that the query planner must evaluate per row, increasing CPU consumption on the processing node above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the UNION ALL path (line 122) with subqueries that each use ORDER BY token_id desc, serial_number desc and LIMIT $2, where the limit is set to a large value, causing the database to materialize and sort large intermediate result sets for each subquery before merging them in the outer query, consuming disproportionate memory and CPU on the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user invoke getNfts() with a query that produces a three-way UNION ALL where each subquery's LEFT JOIN on entity (line 33-34: left join entity e on e.id = nft.token_id) forces three separate hash join or nested loop operations against the entity table, multiplying the join cost by 3x per request and increasing processing node resource consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where the conditions array in getSubQuery() (line 57-63) grows very large due to many filter entries in the filters array, causing the resulting WHERE clause string to be extremely long, increasing SQL parsing and planning time on the database processing node and contributing to a sustained resource consumption increase above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set ownerAccountId to a value that matches a very large number of NFTs in the nft table, then trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path, causing each subquery to scan and return a large number of rows before the outer LIMIT is applied, resulting in the database processing far more rows than necessary and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spenderIdInFilters array with the maximum number of allowed entries to getSubQuery(), where the resulting IN clause (line 72: spender in ($5,$6,...)) is replicated in each of the three subqueries, causing the database to evaluate a large IN list three times per request and increasing CPU consumption on the processing node above the 30% threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where the lower array contains a token_id range filter that forces a partial index scan, the inner array contains a broad token_id range, and the upper array contains another partial range, causing three separate index range scans on the nft table that together cover the entire table, effectively performing a full table scan split across three subqueries with additional join and sort overhead?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNft()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNft() with tokenId and serialNumber values that correspond to NFTs with very large metadata fields, causing nftByIdQuery (select * from nft) to return rows with large binary metadata payloads, increasing network transfer and memory allocation on the processing node per request and contributing to resource consumption increases above 30% when called at a moderate rate?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filters.push(...spenderIdFilters) mutates the filters array in-place (line 56) and that the same spenderIdFilters reference is passed to all three getSubQuery() calls, to craft a scenario where spenderIdFilters contains many entries that are appended to each subquery's conditions, causing each subquery to have a disproportionately large WHERE clause that degrades query planning performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path where each subquery independently applies ORDER BY token_id, serial_number (two-column sort) and LIMIT, and the outer query also applies the same ORDER BY and LIMIT (line 122), causing the database to perform four total sort operations (three inner + one outer) per request, consuming significantly more CPU than a single-subquery request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply filter values for serial_number using boundary operators (gt, gte, lt, lte) that create open-ended ranges, causing each subquery to scan a large portion of the nft table's serial_number index without an upper bound, resulting in large intermediate result sets that must be sorted and limited, increasing I/O and CPU on the processing node above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where the params array (line 86: const params = [ownerAccountId, limit]) grows to a very large size as filters from lower, inner, upper, spenderIdFilters, and spenderIdInFilters are all appended across three getSubQuery() calls, causing the database driver to allocate and bind a large parameter array per request, increasing memory pressure on the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getNfts()] Can an unprivileged external user invoke getNfts() with a query that produces the maximum-complexity SQL (three UNION ALL subqueries, each with LEFT JOIN, ORDER BY, and LIMIT), where the resulting SQL string itself is very long, causing increased parsing overhead in the database engine and contributing to a sustained resource consumption increase above 30% per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft filter conditions where the token_id column filter uses an operator that prevents index usage (e.g., a not-equal or complex expression), causing the nft table scan in each subquery to be a full sequential scan, and when three such subqueries are UNION ALL'd together, tripling the sequential scan cost and increasing processing node I/O above the 30% threshold?
